name: Los Angeles County v. Rettele
question: Were an individual's Fourth Amendment rights violated when the police temporarily detained him in his home despite the fact that his race differed from the race of the suspect identified in the search warrant?
description: A case in which the Court decided that a search conducted on a home when the race of the occupants does not match the race of the supposed occupants identified in the search warrant does not violate the occupant’s Fourth Amendment rights.
